metadata.summary: analysis tool for validating pmac motion controller configuration

documentation:
[![CI](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/dls-pmacanalyse.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/dls-pmacanalyse)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)

# dls_pmacanalyse

A library and CLI for verifying that a delta tau motor controller
configuration matches its motion area definition.

Source          | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse>
:---:           | :---:
PyPI            | `pip install dls-pmacanalyse`
Releases        | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmacanalyse/releases>

functions.name: dls_pmacanalyse.utils.tokenIsInt
documentation: Returns true if the token is an integer.

functions.name: dls_pmacanalyse.utils.tokenIsFloat
documentation: Returns true if the token is a floating point number.

functions.name: dls_pmacanalyse.utils.numericSplit
documentation:
Splits a into two parts, a numeric suffix (or 0 if none) and an
alphanumeric prefix (the remainder).  The parts are returned
as a tuple.

functions.name: dls_pmacanalyse.utils.numericSort
documentation: Used by the sort algorithm to get numeric suffixes in the right order.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmaclexer.PmacLexer.expandToken
documentation: If the token is a short form, it is expanded to the full form.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmaclexer.PmacLexer.getToken
documentation: Returns the first token and removes it from the list.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmaclexer.PmacLexer.putToken
documentation: Puts a token at the head of the list.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmacparser.PmacParser.onLine
documentation: Top level on-line command mode parser.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmacparser.PmacParser.parseExpression
documentation: Returns the result of the expression.
def parseExpression(self):
'''Returns the result of the expression.'''
# Currently only supports a constant prefixed by an optional minus sign
negative = False
t = self.lexer.getToken()
negative = True
t = self.lexer.getToken()
if not tokenIsFloat(t):
raise ParserError('Unsupported', t)
result = tokenToFloat(t)
if negative:
result = -result
return result

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmacparser.PmacParser.parseE1
documentation: Returns the result of a sub-expression containing multiplicative operands.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmacparser.PmacParser.parseE2
documentation: Returns the result of a sub-expression containing monadic operands.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmacparser.PmacParser.parseE3
documentation:
Returns the result of a sub-expression that is an I,P,Q or M variable or
a constant or a parenthesised expression.

classes.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.htmlMotorIVariables
documentation: def readCurrentPositions(self):
"""Read the current motor positions of the PMAC."""
text = ""
for axis in range(self.numAxes):
(returnStr, status) = self.sendCommand("#%sP" % (axis + 1))
self.initialPositions[axis + 1] = returnStr
text += "%s " % returnStr[:-2]
log.info(text)

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readHardware
documentation:
Loads the current state of the PMAC.  If a backupDir is provided, the
state is written as it is read.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.verifyCurrentPositions
documentation: Checks the axis current positions to see if any have moved.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readCurrentPositions
documentation: Returns the current position as a list.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.determinePmacType

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.determineNumAxes
documentation:
Determines the number of axes the PMAC has by determining the
number of macro station ICs.

documentation:
Determines the number of coordinate systems that are active by
reading i68.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readKinematicPrograms
documentation:
Reads the kinematic programs.  Note that this
function will fail if a program exceeds 1350 characters and small buffers
are required.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.getListingLines
documentation:
Returns the listing of a motion program or PLC using
small blocks.  It uses the start and length parameters
of the list command to slowly build up the listing.  Note
that the function fails if any chuck exceeds 1350 characters.
For use in small buffer mode.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readPlcPrograms
documentation: Reads the PLC programs

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readMotionPrograms
documentation:
Reads the motion programs. Note
that only the first 256 programs are read, there are actually 32768.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readMsIvars
documentation: Reads the macrostation I variables.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.readGlobalMsIvars
documentation: Reads the global macrostation I variables.

methods.name: dls_pmacanalyse.pmac.Pmac.doMsIvars
